channel.title: imlucas.dev
channel.description: Software engineer sharing insights on system design, distributed systems, and the patterns behind reliable software.

item.title: How to Design Systems That Handle Millions of Users
item.description: A staged framework for scaling from a single server to a globally distributed architecture, with concrete decision checkpoints, capacity planning math, and observability triggers at every milestone.

item.title: Software Architecture Interview Topics: What to Study and How
item.description: A structured breakdown of the topics architecture interviewers actually test, with representative questions, scoring rubrics, and a realistic two-week prep plan.

item.title: System Design: The Complete Engineer's Guide
item.description: Most system design resources teach you enough to draw boxes on a whiteboard. This guide covers what actually breaks under load and how engineers make real trade-offs in production.

item.title: How Does a Large Language Model Actually Work? An Engineering Perspective
item.description: A ground-up walkthrough of the LLM stack: tokenization, transformer internals, training pipelines, and inference optimization, from an engineering perspective.

item.title: Hello World: Why I Started This Blog
item.description: The signal is live. Here's what to expect from imlucas.dev: AI deep-dives, system design breakdowns, and lessons from the trenches.
